assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md table shows 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 as YES (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0-2.2.4) as NO (h2 >= 0.4.8). Evidence is derived from lock file h2 version numbers at pinned build tags.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md proposes correction to [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1] only. RHTPA 2.2.0 is explicitly removed with reason 'Ships h2 0.4.8 (fixed version) -- NOT affected'. No 2.2.x versions appear in the proposed Affects Versions.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states 'Case A: Affected -- create remediation tasks for the 2.1.x stream only.' Two tasks created: upstream backport on release/0.3.z and downstream propagation in rhtpa-release.0.3.z, both scoped to 2.1.x. Section 'Tasks NOT Created (2.2.x stream)' explicitly confirms no tasks for 2.2.x because all versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8.

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The issue summary... contains no stream suffix in brackets. This issue is therefore unscoped and covers all configured version streams.' and 'Both streams will be analyzed in Step 2.' version-impact.md confirms all 7 versions across both 2.1.x (2.1.0, 2.1.1) and 2.2.x (2.2.0-2.2.4) streams were analyzed.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped -- it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md explicitly states: 'Case B (cross-stream impact) does not apply to unscoped issues -- they cover all streams by definition.' No cross-stream impact notice appears in any output file.
